_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 statewide execs: kim-reynolds(IA gov), adam-gregg(IA former LG), raul-labrador(ID AG), scott-bedke(ID LG), micah-beckwith(IN LG). Currency: Gregg resigned 2024 -> former/not_running; Reynolds not seeking reelection but still sitting gov.

profile.background: First woman governor of Iowa (since 2017); former Clarke County treasurer and state senator.

kim-reynolds.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Currency: announced 2025-04-11 she will NOT seek reelection in 2026; remains sitting governor through Jan 2027. Uniformly conservative documented record: signed 6-week heartbeat abortion ban (2023), defunded Planned Parenthood, trans-athletes ban + 2025 civil-rights-code gender-identity repeal, Students First universal-ESA school choice, RFRA (2024), Back the Blue Act, permitless carry, foreign-farmland restrictions, deployed Guard to TX border. No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: Signed 2023 6-week fetal-heartbeat ban; NRLC named her 'a champion for the right to life'

1.note: Signed a 6-week restriction, not abortion abolition; no abolition advocacy found

2.note: No public position on embryonic stem cell / IVF discard found

3.note: No public position on euthanasia / assisted suicide found

4.note: Defunded Planned Parenthood; SBA Pro-Life America endorsed her — not abortion-industry funded

0.note: No explicit one-man-one-woman marriage statement found

1.note: No public position on same-sex marriage/civil unions found

2.note: Signed trans-athletes ban (2022) and 2025 law removing gender identity from civil-rights code, redefining sex, ending birth-certificate sex changes

3.note: No position on no-fault divorce reform found

4.note: Signed civil-rights-code gender-identity repeal + trans-athletes ban

0.note: Signed Students First Act — universal ESA school choice (2023)

2.note: Cut early voting 29->20 days but did not move to single-day voting

4.note: No cited Zuckerbucks-ban action by her this pass

3.note: 'The feds don't get to run the state of Iowa' — state sovereignty

2.note: State executive; no NFA/GCA-repeal position found

3.note: No castle-doctrine action attributable to her found

profile.background: Iowa Lt. Governor 2017–2024; Iowa State Public Defender 2014–2017; 2014 GOP nominee for Iowa AG (lost). Resigned LG Sept 2024 to become president/CEO of the Iowa Bankers Association.

adam-gregg.notes_set: Currency fix 2026-06: Gregg RESIGNED as Lt. Governor effective Sept 2024 to lead the Iowa Bankers Association (private trade group); replaced by Chris Cournoyer (Dec 2024). Not currently in office and not a 2026 candidate -> marked former/not_running. As LG his role supported Gov. Reynolds's agenda; signed legislation belongs to Reynolds, not Gregg. No independent on-record issue positions or issue-group ratings located — all cells null (no fabrication).

profile.background: Idaho Attorney General since 2023; U.S. Rep. ID-01 2011–2019; founding member of the House Freedom Caucus; Puerto Rico-born former immigration attorney.

raul-labrador.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Sitting Idaho AG, running for reelection 2026 (unopposed GOP primary). Litigation-aggressive record: defended near-total abortion ban at SCOTUS (Moyle), defended HB71 trans-minor-care ban (Poe v. Labrador), defended HB710 library law + HB93 school-choice tax credit, 100+ multistate suits vs federal mandates (Title IX, vaccine mandates, EPA/WOTUS, ATF pistol-brace), co-led parole-in-place immigration suit. Congressional Heritage Action 92%. DEVIATION NOTE: in Congress he was an immigration MODERATE/reform negotiator (Gang-of-8-adjacent) before hardening — deportation cells held null. Election-integrity record thin and his marquee RCV-initiative suit LOST.

0.note: Defended Idaho's near-total abortion ban at SCOTUS (Moyle); Freedom Caucus pro-life, Heritage Action 92%

4.note: Consistent pro-life record defending total abortion ban — not abortion-industry funded

profile.background: Idaho Lt. Governor since 2023; rancher from Oakley; Speaker of the Idaho House 2012–2022; House member 2001–2022.

scott-bedke.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Sitting Idaho LG, running for reelection 2026. MIXED record with documented cross-party deviations (Idaho Freedom Foundation published 'Top 6 Reasons Scott Bedke Isn't a Conservative'): as Speaker he was an OBSTACLE to private-school-choice/ESA (passed only after he left), helped implement a 2013 Obamacare state insurance exchange, and routed/delayed the constitutional-carry bill to an ad-hoc committee. Genuine conservative anchors: 100% Idaho Chooses Life record + helped enact the abortion trigger law; A+ NRA rating + NRA endorsement (guns generally). As Speaker he did not cast floor votes on most marriage/trans bills, so those are null. FALSEs cited: school choice [0], federal-strings/Obamacare exchange [1], constitutional carry [0].

profile.background: Indiana Lt. Governor since Jan 2025 (term to 2029); evangelical pastor at Life Church, Noblesville; former exec director of Indiana Family Action PAC; won 2024 GOP LG nomination at convention. Self-identifies as a 'Christian nationalist.'

micah-beckwith.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06 via social-media/public-statement protocol (no legislative voting record). Sitting Indiana LG to 2029. Outspoken social conservative with rich HARD self-statements: abolitionist on abortion (opposed Indiana's ban as too lenient), anti-trans/LGBTQ, universal school choice, Christianity 'integrated into governance' + backs Ten Commandments in schools, Penny Plan balanced budget, hardline immigration (wall + E-Verify + deny sanctuary funds), 2A absolutist ('shall not be infringed... CANNOT be infringed'), backs death penalty/firing squads. SQUISH FLAGS: floated a narrow rape exception; election-integrity and police records thinner than his loud brand (his election commentary is about Indiana's convention-nomination system, not 2020 fraud) — those cells held null.
